diagrams.title: Singly Linked List Node Structure
diagrams.description: Shows the structure of a single node with data field and next pointer field
elements: Next Pointer Field (arrow pointing to next node)
Head pointer pointing to first node
diagrams.purpose: Understanding basic node composition and pointer concept in singly linked lists

diagrams.title: Insertion at Beginning
diagrams.description: Step-by-step visualization of inserting a new node at the beginning of linked list
elements: Original list with head pointing to Node A
New node created with data X
Step 1: New node's next points to Node A
Step 2: Head pointer updated to point to new node
Result: New node becomes first node
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how head pointer manipulation works for insertion at beginning in O(1)

diagrams.title: Doubly Linked List Structure
diagrams.description: Shows bidirectional pointers in a doubly linked list
elements: Each node has three parts: prev pointer, data, next pointer
First node's prev points to NULL
Last node's next points to NULL
Arrows show both forward and backward connections
diagrams.purpose: Understanding bidirectional traversal capability of doubly linked lists

diagrams.title: Circular Linked List
diagrams.description: Comparison of circular singly linked and doubly linked lists
elements: In circular singly: last node's next points back to head
In circular doubly: last node's next points to head, head's prev points to last
No NULL pointers in circular variants
diagrams.purpose: Understanding circular connection and difference from linear linked lists
